role: This file is the shared core engine that streams Claude turns over NDJSON and batches route-back territory writes for both per-unit gallery brains and the global V/RHM brain.
capabilities: Stream a Claude turn via POST /api/claude/turn and parse NDJSON {init,text,tool,point,done,error} events
Maintain per-address session continuity in a client-side _sessions map
Strip common Markdown syntax from every emitted text delta and final reply
Read live on-screen pointables and dispatch ui:point when the model returns a point token
Batch direction items by element_id and POST /api/territory/write
Suppress writes in ?verify verification mode and broadcast gallery:write-suppressed
Emit gallery:rerender on successful writes and gallery:write-error on failures
Toggle a .brain-loading class and a 'Looking…' wait state
substance_why: The file contains a complete IIFE implementation of streaming fetch parsing, session storage, markdown stripping, pointable dispatch, batched territory writes, verification guards, and fail-loud error events.
interactions_present: Send a prompt and stream the reply into a DOM element or custom onText hook
Display a 'Looking…' wait state and .brain-loading class until the first token arrives
Dispatch ui:point when the model points at an on-screen token
Persist annotation/direction items via batched territory writes
Broadcast gallery:rerender after successful writes and gallery:write-error on failures
Suppress writes and broadcast gallery:write-suppressed when ?verify is present
interactions_missing: No AbortController or cancel/timeout for the long-running turn fetch
No HTTP status check on the /api/claude/turn response before entering the reader loop
No retry/backoff or recovery path for transient network or server errors
No onInit hook exposed to callers
No handling of malformed non-JSON lines beyond silent skip
No UI affordance for verification suppression beyond console warnings and custom events
performance_risks: Markdown stripping runs a battery of global regexes over the entire accumulated string on every text delta, giving O(n²) behavior for long replies
Repeated string concatenation of the NDJSON buffer and accumulated reply without size limits
Promise.all in writeDirections can fire many concurrent fetches for large direction bursts
No timeout or backpressure on the streaming reader
Regex patterns such as /\*\*([\s\S]+?)\*\*/g can be expensive on large inputs
reuse_why: The concepts and contracts are well-scoped and worth keeping, but the implementation should be rebuilt as modular code with abortable streams, a separate markdown sanitizer, and proper HTTP error handling.
decomposition_hints: Split the NDJSON stream reader and turn orchestration into its own module
Split the Markdown stripper into a reusable, testable utility module
Split the per-address session store into a small state module
Split the territory write batcher and verification guard into a persistence module
Split pointable catalog resolution and ui:point dispatch into a surface-binding module
Keep the public window.forkBrainCore API as a thin facade over the modules
